memes.label: "Abolitionism"
memes.description: "The 18th and 19th century movement to abolish the transatlantic slave trade and emancipate enslaved people, chronicled extensively in Jules Verne's Dick Sands."

memes.label: Angola
memes.description: A Portuguese colony and major hub of the 19th-century Atlantic slave trade on the western coast of Africa, where key events of the novel unfold.

memes.label: Atlantic Ocean
memes.description: The Atlantic Ocean, a key geographical feature in the novel — both as the intended destination of the *Pilgrim*'s voyage and as the ultimate goal of the survivors' overland trek across Africa.

memes.label: Auckland
memes.description: "A major New Zealand port city, the departure point of the Pilgrim at the beginning of Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain — where the Weldon family boarded and Negoro joined the crew."

memes.label: Cousin Benedict
memes.description: Cousin Benedict is Mrs. Weldon's eccentric entomologist cousin, whose obsessive pursuit of insects provides both comic relief and unintended consequences throughout the journey.

memes.label: Benguela
memes.description: "A coastal province and port city of Angola, one of the three provinces of Portuguese West Africa, referenced in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain as a key geographic marker and transit point."

memes.label: "Cannibalism"
memes.description: "The practice of eating human flesh, a terrifying reality of central Africa that the protagonists confront in Jules Verne's Dick Sands, representing the ultimate horror of the 'Dark Continent.'"

memes.label: Cape Horn
memes.description: "The southernmost headland of South America, an iconic navigation landmark that the disabled Pilgrim unknowingly rounded — carrying the survivors from the Pacific into the Atlantic in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: "Captain Hull"
memes.description: "Captain of the whaling brig Pilgrim, an experienced seaman whose death in a whaleboat catastrophe leaves Dick Sands to take command of the vessel."

memes.label: Coanza River
memes.description: "One of the principal rivers of Angola, a critical geographic waypoint on the slave caravan's overland journey to Kazonndé in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: "Compass"
memes.description: "The navigational instrument whose sabotage by Negoro sends the Pilgrim off course toward Africa instead of South America, a pivotal plot device in the novel."

memes.label: Congo River
memes.description: "A major African river, also known as the Zaire or Lualaba, at whose mouth the surviving party hoped to find passage home after escaping the slave caravan in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: "Courage"
memes.description: "The quality of facing danger without fear, the defining virtue of the young hero Dick Sands and his companions in Jules Verne's adventure novel."

memes.label: "Crew of the Pilgrim"
memes.description: "The complement of officers and sailors aboard the whaling brig Pilgrim under Captain Hull, whose loss at sea forces fifteen-year-old Dick Sands to assume command."

memes.label: Crocodile
memes.description: The crocodile, a deadly predator of African rivers that threatens the slave caravan during a harrowing river crossing.

memes.label: "Dick Sands"
memes.description: "The fifteen-year-old orphan apprentice who becomes captain of the Pilgrim after disaster strikes, the protagonist of Jules Verne's 1878 adventure novel."

memes.label: "Dingo"
memes.description: "The loyal and mysterious dog rescued along with the African passengers, whose instincts and recognition of Negoro play a crucial role in the novel's plot."

memes.label: "Dingo's Collar"
memes.description: "The collar worn by Dingo bearing engraved letters that provide a crucial clue to the dog's past and Negoro's true identity."

memes.label: "Discovery of the Betrayal"
memes.description: "The moment when Dick Sands and the party realize they have been betrayed by Harris and are in Africa, not South America—the novel's central revelation."

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: "The moral and professional obligation to fulfill one's responsibilities, the ethical backbone of Dick Sands' leadership and the crew's loyalty in Jules Verne's novel."

memes.label: Easter Island
memes.description: "A remote Pacific island, also known as Vai-Hoo, sighted by the drifting Pilgrim in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain — a sighting that raised hopes of landfall before proving fatally deceptive."

memes.label: Elephant
memes.description: The massive pachyderm of the African interior whose trails and tracks serve as natural highways for Dick Sands and his party during their trek across Angola.

memes.label: "Entomology"
memes.description: "The scientific study of insects, the consuming passion of Cousin Benedict in Jules Verne's Dick Sands, providing both comic relief and moments of unexpected usefulness."

memes.label: Escape from the Slavers
memes.description: The perilous flight of Dick Sands and his companions from the slave caravan and their journey toward the coast of Angola, culminating in rescue.

memes.label: "Exploration"
memes.description: "The 19th-century European exploration of the African interior, referenced extensively in Jules Verne's Dick Sands as the historical context for the protagonists' journey through unknown territory."

memes.label: "Freedom"
memes.description: "The state of being free from bondage or constraint, the ultimate aspiration of the enslaved characters and the moral goal of the abolitionist movement in Jules Verne's Dick Sands."

memes.label: Giraffe
memes.description: The giraffe, a key animal in the novel's dramatic reversal when Harris's deception is exposed, appearing in a herd that Dick Sands correctly identifies.

memes.label: "Harris"
memes.description: "An American who pretends to be a guide in South America but is a slave-trader conspiring with Negoro to lead the survivors into the African interior."

memes.label: Hercules
memes.description: A powerfully-built African sailor aboard the Pilgrim, known for his immense strength and loyalty to Dick Sands and Jack Weldon.

memes.label: Hippopotamus
memes.description: The hippopotamus, a recurring presence in the African rivers traversed by the characters, representing the constant environmental dangers of central Africa.

memes.label: Jack Weldon
memes.description: Jack Weldon, the five-year-old son of Mrs. Weldon and James W. Weldon, whose presence adds domestic stakes and childhood innocence to the perilous journey.

memes.label: "James Weldon"
memes.description: "A wealthy San Francisco ship-owner and merchant, husband to Mrs. Weldon and father of Jack Weldon, whose commercial empire sets the novel in motion and whose appearance at the conclusion provides the long-awaited family reunion."

memes.label: Lake Tanganyika
memes.description: "One of the African Great Lakes, a major slave trade hub centered at Ujiji on its shores, and the site of the famous Livingstone–Stanley meeting — all referenced in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: Lion
memes.description: The apex predator of the African savanna, encountered by Dick Sands and his party during their journey across Angola, representing the dangers of the African wilderness.

memes.label: Saint Paul de Loanda
memes.description: "The capital of Portuguese Angola, a major Atlantic port and colonial administrative center that serves as a key geographic reference point in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: "Loss of Captain Hull"
memes.description: "The catastrophic whale hunt in which Captain Hull and the Pilgrim's crew perish, forcing fifteen-year-old Dick Sands to assume command of the vessel in the middle of the Pacific Ocean."

memes.label: Monkey
memes.description: Monkeys appear in the novel's African forest sequences, most notably in Chapter 23's howler-monkey chorus and as a visual simile for human decrepitude.

memes.label: Mossâmedes
memes.description: "A small port in southern Angola that becomes the climactic destination in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain — the site of a desperate race between the escaping survivors and the treacherous Negoro."

memes.label: "Mrs. Weldon"
memes.description: "Wife of wealthy California shipowner James W. Weldon, mother of little Jack, and passenger aboard the Pilgrim whose strength and moral authority sustain the survivors through their African ordeal."

memes.label: "Nan"
memes.description: "The elderly African American servant to the Weldon family who accompanies Mrs. Weldon and young Jack on the voyage of the Pilgrim, providing steadfast care through shipwreck and the African ordeal."

memes.label: "Navigation"
memes.description: "The art and science of directing a vessel's course at sea, a central skill in the novel and the key to Dick Sands' capability as acting captain of the Pilgrim."

memes.label: "Negoro"
memes.description: "The Portuguese cook aboard the Pilgrim who is secretly a slave-trader and the hidden antagonist whose treachery leads the survivors into Africa."

memes.label: Ostrich
memes.description: The ostrich, a large flightless bird that Harris falsely claims the travelers have seen, attempting to disguise the unmistakable African giraffes as something plausible in South America.

memes.label: Parrot
memes.description: Grey parrots appear in the novel's forest passages, most notably as a noisy colony in Chapter 23 and as an object of Cousin Benedict's entomological distraction.

memes.label: "Rescue"
memes.description: "The act of saving someone from danger, a recurring narrative pattern in Jules Verne's Dick Sands that opens the novel and drives its moral stakes."

memes.label: "Rescue of the Africans"
memes.description: "The rescue of five African passengers from a derelict vessel by the Pilgrim, the inciting event that brings Tom, Bat, Austin, Actaeon, and Hercules into the story."
aliases: rescue of Tom Bat Austin Actaeon Hercules

memes.label: "Right Whale"
memes.description: "The species of baleen whale prized by 19th-century whalers, whose increasing scarcity forms the economic backdrop of the Pilgrim's disappointing voyage."

memes.label: San Francisco
memes.description: "The home port of the schooner Pilgrim and the California city to which Mrs. Weldon and her party are bound after their voyage from New Zealand."

memes.label: "Ship's Log"
memes.description: "The navigational instrument used to measure a ship's speed and distance traveled, one of the two instruments Dick Sands relies upon to navigate the Pilgrim."

memes.label: "Shipwreck"
memes.description: "The destruction or foundering of a vessel at sea, a pivotal event in Jules Verne's Dick Sands when the Pilgrim's crew is lost and the young apprentice must assume command."

memes.label: "Slave Caravan"
memes.description: "The organized overland transport of enslaved captives across Africa, the mechanism of the internal slave trade that the protagonists witness and experience in Jules Verne's Dick Sands."

memes.label: "Slavery"
memes.description: "The institution of slavery and the transatlantic slave trade as depicted in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain (1878), a central theme that drives the novel's second half."

memes.label: "Stag Beetle"
memes.description: "A species of beetle in the family Lucanidae, part of Cousin Benedict's extensive entomological collection and the object of his scientific passion in Jules Verne's novel."

memes.label: "Storm at Sea"
memes.description: "The violent oceanic storms in Jules Verne's Dick Sands that test the crew's seamanship and ultimately drive the Pilgrim off course toward the African coast."

memes.label: Termite
memes.description: The industrious insect of the African interior, whose massive mounds serve as landmarks and whose destructive power fascinates Cousin Benedict during the trek across Angola.

memes.label: "The Pilgrim"
memes.description: "The whaling brig owned by James W. Weldon and commanded by Captain Hull, whose voyage from New Zealand to San Francisco goes disastrously off course after sabotage by the cook Negoro."

memes.label: "Tom"
memes.description: "An elderly African American who serves as the spokesman and moral elder of the five Africans rescued from a slave ship, providing wisdom and steadfast counsel to Dick Sands throughout the ordeal in Africa."

memes.label: "Treachery"
memes.description: "The act of betrayal that drives the central plot twist in Jules Verne's Dick Sands, where trusted guides prove to be slave-traders leading the party into captivity."

memes.label: Valparaíso
memes.description: "A major Chilean port city on the Pacific coast, the intended destination of the Pilgrim for unloading cargo — the safe harbor the disabled ship never reached in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain."

memes.label: "Weldon & Co."
memes.description: "The San Francisco-based whaling and shipping firm owned by James W. Weldon, whose vessels operate in both Arctic and Antarctic waters."

memes.label: "Whaling"
memes.description: "The commercial hunting of whales for oil and whalebone, the industry that drives the Weldon family business and sets the stage for the voyage of the Pilgrim in Jules Verne's Dick Sands."

memes.label: Zambesi River
memes.description: "A major river of east Africa, explored by David Livingstone and referenced in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain as part of the geographic framework connecting the African interior to the Indian Ocean slave trade."

memes.label: Zanzibar
memes.description: "A major East African island and hub of the 19th-century Indian Ocean slave trade, frequently referenced in Jules Verne's Dick Sands: The Boy Captain as both a slave-market destination and a base for European exploration of the African interior."
